title: rba / rate decision / cash rate | Congressional Testimony | Opening Statement to the House of Representatives Standing C...
summary: RBA Governor Michele Bullock testified before the House of Representatives Standing Committee on Economics, confirming the Monetary Policy Board's decision earlier that week to raise the cash rate target by 25 basis points to 3.85%, citing persistent inflation pressures. Underlying inflation rose to 3.4% in Q4 2025 from 2.7% in mid-2025, with the RBA now projecting inflation won't return to the 2-3% target band until mid-2027. The Board cited stronger-than-expected domestic demand, resilient global growth, and easing financial conditions as factors driving the inflation pickup.
